workItem.title: Make workspace recovery atomic and single-owner

## Context

During a controlled Symphony restart, a long-running Issue was recovered through overlapping eligibility/recovery paths. Its workspace was left without .git and with only ignored root-owned test artifacts. The candidate commit remained safe on the remote Issue branch, but the active Codex session started in an invalid directory.

## Expected outcome

Exactly one recovery path owns each Issue workspace, validates or atomically reconstructs it from the confirmed remote candidate, quarantines incomplete directories and never starts Codex without a valid repository at the expected commit.

## Scope

- Acquire a per-Issue workspace lock before cleanup, clone or resume
- Coalesce tracker eligibility and local recovery for the same Issue
- Validate .git, repository identity, branch and exact HEAD before App Server startup
- Atomically quarantine invalid or partially removed workspaces
- Reclone from the durable remote Issue branch and verify the expected candidate SHA
- Handle root-owned ignored test artifacts without leaving a partial directory
- Record recovery and quarantine events durably

## Out of scope

- Delete quarantined directories automatically
- Recover local-only commits that were never published
- Use a second canonical workspace database

## Acceptance criteria

- Concurrent recovery attempts for one Issue result in one workspace and one Codex session
- Codex never starts when .git is absent or repository identity is wrong
- An invalid non-empty workspace is atomically quarantined before clone
- A root-owned ignored cache cannot leave the canonical workspace partially removed
- Recovery checks out the exact confirmed remote candidate commit
- Restart tests cover stale tracker projection plus an existing local recovery candidate
- No AgentRun, Attempt, PR or workspace is duplicated
- Complete Symphony recovery and quality gates pass
